what_it_does: Sets window.CV_VI_GLYPH to a single data object holding the ConceptV 'Vi' wordmark as auto-traced SVG path data: a viewBox string, width/height (120x114), and a multi-subpath 'path' string (5 subpaths: V blades, chevron, i-stem, i-dot). Consumed by the shape renderer (cv-shapes.js) to draw the Vi mark in a single fill colour.
observations: Pure data file — a two-line comment header plus one assignment of a JSON object literal to a window global.
Comment states it was AUTO-TRACED from assets/brand-marks/vi.png and is 'the ONE home for the Vi glyph'.
path uses fill-rule even-odd implicitly (drawn with fill-rule='evenodd' by the consumer per cv-shapes.js).
viewBox '0 0 120 114' equals the w/h fields; the bbox is the glyph itself.
standouts: Declares itself the single canonical home for the Vi glyph, drawn in one fill colour by the entity-shape renderer.
conventions: window global publish pattern (no module.exports here, unlike cv-shapes.js).
Single-source discipline — one home for the glyph, referenced by cv-shapes.js viWordmark().
Provenance noted inline (traced from a named source PNG).
notes: A one-line data module: the traced Vi wordmark path used by the shape renderer's wordmark layer.
questions: Is the traced path expected to be regenerated if assets/brand-marks/vi.png changes, and by what tool?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it is exactly the single data home for the Vi glyph that its comment claims to be.
intention_signals: Trying to keep the brand wordmark as one reusable vector asset so any consumer draws an identical Vi from one canonical path rather than embedding copies.
novelty: An auto-traced raster-to-vector brand mark stored as plain JS data with viewBox metadata for renderer-agnostic reuse.
contribution: Contributes the canonical Vi wordmark vector path data with its bounding-box metadata for single-colour rendering.
summary_for_embedding: A JavaScript data module setting window.CV_VI_GLYPH to the ConceptV 'Vi' wordmark as auto-traced SVG path data with viewBox and width/height, for single-fill rendering by the shape renderer.
intention_for_embedding: To hold the Vi brand glyph as one canonical vector asset so every consumer renders an identical wordmark.
